nameMeanings.name: Moels
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Moll , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Catalan: habitational name from any of various minor places called Moles, from the plural of mola (see Mola ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Meulles (Calvados). See also   Moyles  .

variants.name: Moll
meanings: English (Norfolk): from the Middle English female personal name Moll(e), Mol, a common pet form of Maud (see Mould and Maude ).

English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English molle, mol(e), moul ‘mole’ (the burrowing animal), given perhaps to someone who wore moleskin, a mole catcher, or to someone with poor sight, or alternatively from Middle English mol ‘effeminate man’.
German: nickname from a dialect term for a plump, stout person. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland.

nameMeanings.name: Johnana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Johnana Moels was born before 1305, her father, Roger de Moels, was 33 and her mother, Alice Prouz, was -6. She had at least 3 sons with John de Wotton. She died after 13 October 1343, in Honiton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johnana Moels est née avant 1305, son père, Roger de Moels, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Alice Prouz, avait -6 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils avec John de Wotton. Elle est décédée après 13 octobre 1343, à Honiton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johnana Moels vor 1305 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Moels 33 und ihre Mutter Alice Prouz -6. Sie hatte mit John de Wotton mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb nach 13. Oktober 1343 in Honiton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johnana Moels è nata il prima del 1305, sua padre, Roger de Moels, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Alice Prouz, aveva -6 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli con John de Wotton. Morì dopo il 13 ottobre 1343, nel Honiton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Johnana Moels(이)가 1305년 이전에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Moels(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Prouz(은)는 -6세였다. 그녀는 John de Wotton(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1343년 10월 13일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 호니턴에서 사망했다.
